key_insight: The paper uses transferable neural networks to predict adaptive coarse spaces for overlapping domain decomposition methods, removing the need to solve costly local eigenvalue problems online.
engineering_relevance: This can significantly speed up robust PDE solvers for highly heterogeneous and nonlinear problems while preserving scalability and coefficient-contrast robustness.
method: Regression networks predict adaptive coarse basis functions.
A classification network predicts how many basis functions are needed for robustness.
Sign-invariant losses and post-processing improve prediction quality; the learned model transfers across diffusion, elasticity, and p-Laplace cases without retraining.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluation on high-contrast scalar diffusion and transfer tests to linear elasticity and nonlinear p-Laplace problems, including a nonlinear Schwarz framework.
watch_item: Predictions must remain reliable across problem classes and coefficient contrasts, so sign handling and post-processing are important implementation details.
paper_title: Learning Adaptive Coarse Spaces Using Transferable Neural Network Models for Linear and Nonlinear Overlapping Domain Decomposition Methods
